title: Protocol for abscisic acid (ABA) extraction from plant seeds
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"io0c","text":"The plant hormone abscisic acid (ABA) regulates seed dormancy and germination. Here, we present a protocol for ABA extraction from plant seeds. We describe necessary steps required for preparation and extraction, followed by liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) analysis for ABA quantification.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9o22i","text":"\n\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"44o71","text":"To prepare before to start:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"70sme","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5s078","text":"Prepare   of working solution per sample: 100% Methanol spiked with   D6-ABA stock solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":8,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":68,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"2","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":2}},"1":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"1","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1800}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5johc","text":"List of equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"22o0s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8hrqm","text":"Calibrated balance, ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f5etr","text":"Grinder (Mixer Mill MM 400, RETSCH‱), ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":34,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"enu81","text":"Sonicator (Branson 5510R-DTH Ultrasonic machine), ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"chn5e","text":"ultra-centrifuge (Centrifuge 5424 R, EppendorfTM), ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"698te","text":"speedVac (Concentrator Plus, EppendorfTM), ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ads6t","text":"0.2 um filter (Non-Sterile Syringe Filter 13 mm; WhatmanTM UnifloTM), ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6megi","text":"0.1 mL Micro-insert clear glass tube (fisher scientific), ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"193d7","text":"1.5 mL LCMS vial.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"0A682DFB591E11EE95520A58A9FEAC02","name":":registered:"}}}}
steps: Grind 7-8 seeds in a Safe-Lock 2.0 mL Eppendorf tube with 3-4 beads for 00:01:00 – frequency 25-26 Hz.
Weight 100 mg fine powder of ground seeds.
Add 1 mL of Standard solution in each tube.
Centrifuge for 14000 rpm, , 5 minutes , then transfer the supernatant to a new 2 mL Eppendorf tube.
Add another 1 mL of Standard solution.
Transfer the supernatant ( 2 mL ) to a 2 mL Eppendorf tube.
Dry the supernatant under vacuum by speedVac for 02:00:00 .
After evaporating the solvent, either keep the extracted samples at -20 °C or proceed to the next step.
Re-dissolve the final extract in 120 µL of acetonitrile : water [25:75 (v/v)] followed by 00:01:00 sonication.
Filter the re-suspended solution through a 0.22 µm filter into 0.1 mL micro-insert 29x5.7 mm clear glass tubes with inserted vials.
Tap the bottle to remove any bubbles.
ABA quantification is performed by LC-MS/MS using a UHPLC-Triple-Stage Quadrupole Mass Spectrometer (Thermo Scientific Altis) machine.

Chromatographic separation is achieved on the Hypersil GOLD C18 Selectivity HPLC Columns (150 × 4.6 mm; 3 μm; fisher scientific) with mobile phases consisting of water (A) and acetonitrile (B), both containing 0.1% formic acid, and the following linear gradient (flow rate, 0.5 mL/min): 0–10 min, 15%–100 % B, follow it by washing with 100 % B for 00:05:00 and equilibration with 15 % B for 00:02:00 .
Inject 10 µL of sample, maintain the column temperature at 35 °C for each run.

Set the MS parameters of Thermo ScientificTM AltisTM as follows: negative mode, ion source of H-ESI, ion spray voltage of 3000 V, sheath gas of 40 arbitrary units, aux gas of 15 arbitrary units, sweep gas of 0 arbitrary units, ion transfer tube gas temperature of 350 °C , vaporizer temperature of 350 °C , collision energy of 20 eV, CID gas of 2 mTorr, and full width at half maximum (FWHM) 0.4 Da of Q1/Q3 mass. The characteristic Multiple Reaction Monitoring (MRM) transitions (precursor ion → product ion) were the characteristic MRM transitions (precursor ion → product ion) were 263.2 → 153.1 for ABA; 269.2 → 159.1 for D6-ABA.
creator: Lamis Berqdar
